toolUseResult.originalFile:
# hotloops - hot inner-loop detector

Detects the hottest loops in a RISC-V ELF by observing backward
translation-block transitions in QEMU. For each detected loop you get
the loop header PC, the backward-branch PC, and the iteration count.
With DWARF, you also get the source `file:line`.

Two parts ship together:

- `contrib/plugins/hotloops.c` - QEMU TCG plugin that records edges.
- `scripts/hotloops.sh` - wrapper that runs the plugin and formats output.

## Usage

```
scripts/hotloops.sh [options] <elf> [--] [program args...]
```

### Options

| Flag                | Default | Description                                                    |
|---------------------|---------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `--arch <a>`        | auto    | Target arch: `riscv32`, `riscv64`, or `auto` (from `file`).    |
| `--qemu <path>`     | auto    | Override the QEMU binary.                                      |
| `--plugin <path>`   | auto    | Override the plugin `.so`.                                     |
| `--limit <N>`       | 50      | Print at most N loops.                                         |
| `--min-iters <N>`   | 1       | Drop loops with fewer than N iterations (hot-loop threshold).  |
| `--objdump <path>`  | auto    | Override objdump (for `function+offset` resolution).           |
| `--addr2line <path>`| auto    | Override addr2line (for source line resolution).               |
| `--no-source`       | off     | Skip source resolution even if DWARF is present.               |
| `--raw`             | off     | Print raw plugin output (CSV-ish), no formatting.              |
| `--optimize-rvv`    | on      | Only report loops that contain at least one RVV instruction.   |

### Build prerequisites

The plugin lives in `contrib/plugins/`. Build once:

```
ninja -C build contrib-plugins
```

It is incremental on subsequent edits to `hotloops.c`. The QEMU binaries
themselves (`qemu-riscv64`, `qemu-riscv32`) do **not** need to be rebuilt.

## How it detects a loop

For every translated block, the plugin remembers the PC of its last
instruction. When the next-executed block starts at a PC `<=` that
remembered PC, the transition is a backward edge - i.e. a loop iteration.
The pair `(header_pc, tail_pc)` is the key; the count is the number of
iterations.

This catches every loop a real backward branch closes (for/while/do-while,
goto, manually unrolled tails, etc.) without needing to model branch
semantics.

Caveat: each iteration counted is one *taken* backward branch. A loop
that runs N times has N-1 taken backward branches plus one fall-through
on exit, so the reported count is N-1 per call to the enclosing function.

## Output format

Header line, then a fixed-width table sorted by `iters` descending:

```
# hot loops (iters >= <threshold>), sorted desc, top <K>
iters | start_pc           -> end_pc             | location              | source
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
<n>   | 0x<16-hex>         -> 0x<16-hex>         | <fn>+0x<o> -> <fn>+0x<o> | file:line -> file:line
...
```

Columns:

- `iters`     - taken backward-branch count (sorted desc).
- `start_pc`  - PC of the loop header (the branch target).
- `end_pc`    - PC of the backward branch (the loop tail).
- `location`  - `function+offset -> function+offset` from the ELF symbol table.
                Shows `?` if the address is not inside any sized FUNC symbol.
- `source`    - `file:line -> file:line` from DWARF. Collapsed to a single
                `file:line` when both ends map to the same source line.
                Only present when the ELF has debug info and `--no-source`
                is not set.

The `--raw` mode prints the unformatted plugin output instead:

```
hotloops: <distinct> distinct backward edges, <hot> hot (>= <thr> iters), sorted by iterations desc
start_pc, end_pc, iterations
0x..., 0x..., <n>
...
```

## Example

Source:

```c
/* /tmp/hotloops_demo.c */
#include <stdio.h>

__attribute__((noinline))
static int dot(volatile const int *a, volatile const int *b, int n) {
    int s = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += a[i] * b[i];   /* line 6 */
    return s;
}

int main(void) {
    int a[64], b[64];
    for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) { a[i] = i; b[i] = 64 - i; }
    long total = 0;
    for (int outer = 0; outer < 500; outer++)
        total += dot(a, b, 64);                      /* 64 * 500 inner iters */
    printf("total=%ld\n", total);
    return 0;
}
```

Build with debug info and run:

```
$ riscv64-linux-gnu-gcc -O1 -g -static /tmp/hotloops_demo.c -o /tmp/hotloops_demo
$ scripts/hotloops.sh --limit 5 --min-iters 100 /tmp/hotloops_demo
```

Output:

```
# hot loops (iters >= 100), sorted desc, top 5
iters | start_pc           -> end_pc             | location                                                                | source
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
31500 | 0x000000000001056e -> 0x000000000001057c | dot+0xc -> dot+0x1a                                                     | hotloops_demo.c:6 (discriminator 3) -> hotloops_demo.c:6 (discriminator 1)
 1757 | 0x000000000004ab54 -> 0x000000000004abe8 | read_encoded_value_with_base+0x70 -> read_encoded_value_with_base+0x104 | unwind-pe.h:265 -> unwind-pe.h:255
  876 | 0x000000000004bc5e -> 0x000000000004bd00 | classify_object_over_fdes+0x46 -> classify_object_over_fdes+0xe8        | unwind-dw2-fde.c:738 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:732 (discriminator 1)
  876 | 0x000000000004aae4 -> 0x000000000004bcdc | read_encoded_value_with_base+0x0 -> classify_object_over_fdes+0xc4      | unwind-pe.h:203 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:781
  869 | 0x000000000004bcf2 -> 0x000000000004bd64 | classify_object_over_fdes+0xda -> classify_object_over_fdes+0x14c       | unwind-dw2-fde.c:786 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:793
```

Reading the top row:

- `iters = 31500` - close to the expected `64 * 500 = 32000`; the missing
  500 are the per-call exits (last iter of `dot` falls through instead of
  branching back).
- `start_pc = 0x1056e`, `end_pc = 0x1057c` - the header and backward
  branch of the inner loop.
- `location` - both PCs are inside `dot`, header at `+0xc`, tail at `+0x1a`.
- `source` - both map to `hotloops_demo.c:6`, which is exactly
  `for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += a[i] * b[i];`.

The remaining rows are glibc unwinder loops that ran during static init.
The 500-iter outer loop (`main+0x38 -> main+0x3a`) was filtered out by
`--min-iters 100` - well below the inner loop's contribution.

## Tips

- Strip rarely-interesting libc/startup loops by raising `--min-iters`
  to a value above your background noise floor (e.g. `--min-iters 1000`
  for a workload that runs your hot path millions of times).
- For a very short workload, lower `--min-iters` and raise `--limit`
  to see the full distribution.
- Pass program arguments after `--`, e.g.
  `scripts/hotloops.sh ./prog -- --bench fast`.
- `--raw` is useful when piping into another tool; the format is stable.

lines: | `--no-source`       | off     | Skip source resolution even if DWARF is present.               |
| `--raw`             | off     | Print raw plugin output (CSV-ish), no formatting.              |
| `--optimize-rvv`    | on      | Only report loops that contain at least one RVV instruction.   |
+| `--no-optimize-rvv` | off     | Disable the RVV-only filter; report all loops.                 |

Plugin (`contrib/plugins/hotloops.c`):
- `optimize_rvv=0/1` arg, default 0.
- Per-TB RVV scan at translation: opcode `0x57` (OP-V), or `0x07`/`0x27` with funct3 in `{0,5,6,7}` (vector load/store).
- Per-CPU `saw_rvv` accumulates RVV across TBs, committed to the loop edge on each backward edge, then reset.
- `plugin_exit` filters loops without `has_rvv` when `optimize_rvv=1`.

Script (`scripts/hotloops.sh`):
- `OPTIMIZE_RVV=1` default, `--optimize-rvv` / `--no-optimize-rvv` toggles.
- Passes `optimize_rvv=$OPTIMIZE_RVV` to the plugin.

Docs (`docs/hotloops.md`):
- Added both `--optimize-rvv` (default on) and `--no-optimize-rvv` rows.

Build: clean.

Caveat: the RVV-detection heuristic is approximate. It can falsely mark a loop on its first observed iteration if RVV ran just before the loop entry (the per-CPU `saw_rvv` is not cleared on loop entry, only on backward-edge commit). For long-running hot loops this is rarely user-visible, but worth noting.

The plugin tracks "did I see an RVV instruction?" on a per-CPU flag (`saw_rvv`). The flag is set whenever any TB containing RVV executes, and committed-then-cleared only when a backward edge fires. There's no notion of "loop entry" - so RVV that ran *before* a loop can leak into that loop's first iteration.

Concrete trace:

```
TB_x  (vsetvli + vle32 ...)   <- pre-loop RVV setup
TB_h  (loop header, scalar)   <- loop starts here
TB_b  (loop body, scalar)
TB_t  (loop tail, scalar bne) <- backward branch back to TB_h
```

Step-by-step:
1. `TB_x` runs: `saw_rvv = true`. (No backward edge.)
2. `TB_h` runs: no backward edge yet (first entry). `saw_rvv` still true.
3. `TB_b`, `TB_t` run.
4. `TB_h` runs again - backward edge detected. Commit: `loop.has_rvv = true`. Reset `saw_rvv = false`.

The loop body is pure scalar, but it got marked RVV because pre-loop setup contaminated the flag. `has_rvv` is sticky (boolean OR), so the false mark persists.

When it bites:
- A scalar loop placed immediately after vector setup code (common in vectorized routines: `vsetvli` outside the loop, then a scalar epilogue/prologue loop nearby).
- Tools that mix RVV and scalar phases interleaved.

When it doesn't bite:
- Loops whose body genuinely contains RVV - mark is correct regardless.
- Loops far from any RVV code - `saw_rvv` is false by the time the first backward edge fires, since other backward edges in between cleared it.

A correct fix would track loop entry/exit (e.g., clear `saw_rvv` whenever a *new* loop edge fires for the first time, or maintain an active-loop stack). I skipped it because (a) for hot inner loops with high iteration counts, false positives are rare in practice, and (b) properly attributing RVV to nested loops is a bigger design problem than the MVP needs.

64	        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
65	        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
66	        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
67	        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
68	        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
69	        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
70	        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
71	        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
72	        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
73	        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
74	        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
75	        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
76	        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
77	        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
78	        -h|--help)     usage ;;
79	        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
80	        *)
81	            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
82	                ELF="$1"; shift

85	            fi ;;
86	    esac
87	done
88	
89	[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
90	[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }
91	
92	# --- arch detection ---
93	if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
94	    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"

135	fi

137	# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
138	if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
139	    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
140	                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
141	        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
142	            OBJDUMP="$cand"

144	        fi

150	trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES"' EXIT
151	if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
152	    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
153	    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
154	        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {

161	fi

164	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
165	    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
166	                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
167	                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do

169	            ADDR2LINE="$cand"

171	        fi
172	    done
173	fi

175	# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
176	# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
177	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
178	    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
179	    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
180	        # addr2line: -e <elf> -f -s -C reads addrs on stdin, prints
181	        # alternating function/file:line; we ask for file:line only via -p.
182	        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" -s 2>/dev/null || true)"
183	        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
184	    fi
185	fi
186	
187	# --- format ---
188	python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" <<'PY'
189	import sys, re
190	
191	log_path = sys.argv[1]
192	syms_path = sys.argv[2]
193	lines_path = sys.argv[3]
194	limit = int(sys.argv[4])
195	min_iters = int(sys.argv[5])
196	
197	syms = []
198	try:
199	    with open(syms_path) as f:
200	        for line in f:
201	            parts = line.split()
202	            if len(parts) >= 3:
203	                addr = int(parts[0])
204	                size = int(parts[1])
205	                name = " ".join(parts[2:])
206	                syms.append((addr, size, name))
207	except FileNotFoundError:
208	    pass
209	
210	def resolve(pc):
211	    for addr, size, name in syms:
212	        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
213	            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
214	    return "?"
215	
216	src_map = {}
217	try:
218	    with open(lines_path) as f:
219	        for line in f:
220	            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
221	            if len(parts) == 2:
222	                pc_str, src = parts
223	                src = src.strip()
224	                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
225	                    try:
226	                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
227	                    except ValueError:
228	                        pass
229	except FileNotFoundError:
230	    pass
231	
232	def src_line(pc):
233	    return src_map.get(pc, "")
234	
235	rows = []
236	with open(log_path) as f:
237	    for line in f:
238	        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)", line)
239	        if m:
240	            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
241	                         int(m.group(2), 16),
242	                         int(m.group(3))))
243	
244	if not rows:
245	    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
246	    sys.exit(0)
247	
248	rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
249	rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
250	rows = rows[:limit]
251	
252	if not rows:
253	    sys.stderr.write(f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}\n")
254	    sys.exit(0)
255	
256	print(f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}")
257	
258	have_src = bool(src_map)
259	
260	w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
261	w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
262	w_sym = max(len("location"),
263	            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))
264	
265	if have_src:
266	    def srcpair(s, e):
267	        a, b = src_line(s), src_line(e)
268	        if a and b and a == b:
269	            return a
270	        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
271	    w_src = max(len("source"),
272	                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
273	    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | "
274	          f"{'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
275	    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
276	    for s, e, c in rows:
277	        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
278	        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
279	              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
280	else:
281	    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
282	    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
283	    for s, e, c in rows:
284	        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
285	        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
286	PY

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
#
# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
#
#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
#
# Options:
#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
#                                   (repeatable)
#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
#                                   instruction (default: on)
#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
#   -h | --help                     Show help

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

ARCH="auto"
QEMU=""
PLUGIN=""
LIMIT=50
MIN_ITERS=1
OBJDUMP=""
ADDR2LINE=""
NO_SOURCE=0
RAW=0
OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
MACHINE="andes_ae350"
CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
QEMU_CONFIG=""
QEMU_CWD=""
EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()

usage() { sed -n '2,30p' "$0"; exit 0; }

# --- arg parsing ---
ELF=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
        -h|--help)     usage ;;
        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
        *)
            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
                ELF="$1"; shift
            else
                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
            fi ;;
    esac
done

[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- arch detection ---
if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    case "$file_out" in
        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    esac
fi

# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
fi
[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }

if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
fi
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- run ---
LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT

QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS},optimize_rvv=${OPTIMIZE_RVV}"
          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
          -M "$MACHINE"
          -cpu "$CPU"
          -nographic -semihosting
          -bios "$ELF")
[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
else
    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
fi

if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cat "$LOG"
    exit 0
fi

# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            OBJDUMP="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES"' EXIT
if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
                  name=$NF;
                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
              }' \
        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true
fi

# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
        # addr2line: -e <elf> -f -s -C reads addrs on stdin, prints
        # alternating function/file:line; we ask for file:line only via -p.
        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" -s 2>/dev/null || true)"
        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
    fi
fi

# --- format ---
python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" <<'PY'
import sys, re

log_path = sys.argv[1]
syms_path = sys.argv[2]
lines_path = sys.argv[3]
limit = int(sys.argv[4])
min_iters = int(sys.argv[5])

syms = []
try:
    with open(syms_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.split()
            if len(parts) >= 3:
                addr = int(parts[0])
                size = int(parts[1])
                name = " ".join(parts[2:])
                syms.append((addr, size, name))
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def resolve(pc):
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
    return "?"

src_map = {}
try:
    with open(lines_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
            if len(parts) == 2:
                pc_str, src = parts
                src = src.strip()
                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
                    try:
                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def src_line(pc):
    return src_map.get(pc, "")

rows = []
with open(log_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)", line)
        if m:
            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
                         int(m.group(2), 16),
                         int(m.group(3))))

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
    sys.exit(0)

rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
rows = rows[:limit]

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write(f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}\n")
    sys.exit(0)

print(f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}")

have_src = bool(src_map)

w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
w_sym = max(len("location"),
            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))

if have_src:
    def srcpair(s, e):
        a, b = src_line(s), src_line(e)
        if a and b and a == b:
            return a
        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
    w_src = max(len("source"),
                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | "
          f"{'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
else:
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
PY

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
#
# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
#
#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
#
# Options:
#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
#                                   (repeatable)
#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
#                                   instruction (default: on)
#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
#   -h | --help                     Show help

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

ARCH="auto"
QEMU=""
PLUGIN=""
LIMIT=50
MIN_ITERS=1
OBJDUMP=""
ADDR2LINE=""
NO_SOURCE=0
RAW=0
OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
MACHINE="andes_ae350"
CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
QEMU_CONFIG=""
QEMU_CWD=""
EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()

usage() { sed -n '2,30p' "$0"; exit 0; }

# --- arg parsing ---
ELF=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
        -h|--help)     usage ;;
        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
        *)
            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
                ELF="$1"; shift
            else
                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
            fi ;;
    esac
done

[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- arch detection ---
if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    case "$file_out" in
        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    esac
fi

# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
fi
[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }

if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
fi
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- run ---
LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT

QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS},optimize_rvv=${OPTIMIZE_RVV}"
          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
          -M "$MACHINE"
          -cpu "$CPU"
          -nographic -semihosting
          -bios "$ELF")
[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
else
    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
fi

if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cat "$LOG"
    exit 0
fi

# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            OBJDUMP="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES"' EXIT
if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
                  name=$NF;
                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
              }' \
        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true
fi

# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
        # addr2line: -e <elf> -f -s -C reads addrs on stdin, prints
        # alternating function/file:line; we ask for file:line only via -p.
        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" -s 2>/dev/null || true)"
        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
    fi
fi

# --- format ---
python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" <<'PY'

log_path = sys.argv[1]
syms_path = sys.argv[2]
lines_path = sys.argv[3]
limit = int(sys.argv[4])
min_iters = int(sys.argv[5])

syms = []
try:
    with open(syms_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.split()
            if len(parts) >= 3:
                addr = int(parts[0])
                size = int(parts[1])
                name = " ".join(parts[2:])
                syms.append((addr, size, name))
except FileNotFoundError:

                pc_str, src = parts
                src = src.strip()

                    try:
                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
except FileNotFoundError:

def src_line(pc):
    return src_map.get(pc, "")

rows = []
with open(log_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)", line)
        if m:
            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
                         int(m.group(2), 16),
                         int(m.group(3))))

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
    sys.exit(0)

rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
rows = rows[:limit]

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write(f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}\n")
    sys.exit(0)

print(f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}")

have_src = bool(src_map)

w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
w_sym = max(len("location"),
            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))

if have_src:
    def srcpair(s, e):
        a, b = src_line(s), src_line(e)
        if a and b and a == b:
            return a

                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))

else:
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
    for s, e, c in rows:

                size = int(parts[1])
                name = " ".join(parts[2:])
                syms.append((addr, size, name))
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def resolve(pc):
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
    return "?"

src_map = {}
try:
    with open(lines_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
            if len(parts) == 2:
                pc_str, src = parts
                src = src.strip()
                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
                    try:
                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def src_line(pc):
    return src_map.get(pc, "")

rows = []
with open(log_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)", line)
        if m:
            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
                         int(m.group(2), 16),
                         int(m.group(3))))

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
    sys.exit(0)

rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]
rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
rows = rows[:limit]

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write(f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}\n")
    sys.exit(0)

print(f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}")

have_src = bool(src_map)

w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
w_sym = max(len("location"),
            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))

if have_src:
    def srcpair(s, e):
        a, b = src_line(s), src_line(e)
        if a and b and a == b:
            return a
        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
    w_src = max(len("source"),
                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | "
          f"{'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
else:
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
PY

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
3	#
4	# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
5	# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
6	#
7	#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
8	#
9	# Usage:
10	#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
11	#
12	# Options:
13	#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
14	#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
15	#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
16	#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
17	#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
18	#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
19	#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
20	#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
21	#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
22	#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
23	#                                   (repeatable)
24	#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
25	#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
26	#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
27	#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
28	#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
29	#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
30	#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
31	#                                   instruction (default: on)
32	#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
33	#   -h | --help                     Show help
34	
35	set -euo pipefail
36	
37	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
38	QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
39	
40	ARCH="auto"
41	QEMU=""
42	PLUGIN=""
43	LIMIT=50
44	MIN_ITERS=1
45	OBJDUMP=""
46	ADDR2LINE=""
47	NO_SOURCE=0
48	RAW=0
49	OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
50	MACHINE="andes_ae350"
51	CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
52	QEMU_CONFIG=""
53	QEMU_CWD=""
54	EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()
55	
56	usage() { sed -n '2,30p' "$0"; exit 0; }
57	
58	# --- arg parsing ---
59	ELF=""
60	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
61	    case "$1" in
62	        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
63	        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
64	        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
65	        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
66	        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
67	        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
68	        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
69	        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
70	        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
71	        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
72	        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
73	        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
74	        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
75	        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
76	        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
77	        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
78	        -h|--help)     usage ;;
79	        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
80	        *)
81	            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
82	                ELF="$1"; shift
83	            else
84	                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
85	            fi ;;
86	    esac
87	done
88	
89	[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
90	[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }
91	
92	# --- arch detection ---
93	if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
94	    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
95	    case "$file_out" in
96	        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
97	        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
98	        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
99	    esac
100	fi
101	
102	# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
103	if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
104	    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
105	fi
106	[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }
107	
108	if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
109	    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
110	fi
111	[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }
112	
113	# --- run ---
114	LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
115	trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT
116	
117	QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
118	          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS}"
119	          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
120	          -M "$MACHINE"
121	          -cpu "$CPU"
122	          -nographic -semihosting
123	          -bios "$ELF")
124	[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
125	[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
126	if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
127	    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
128	else
129	    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
130	fi
131	
132	if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
133	    cat "$LOG"
134	    exit 0
135	fi
136	
137	# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
138	if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
139	    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
140	                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
141	        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
142	            OBJDUMP="$cand"
143	            break
144	        fi
145	    done
146	fi
147	
148	SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
149	LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
150	DISASM="$(mktemp -t hotloops_disasm.XXXXXX)"
151	trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM"' EXIT
152	if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
153	    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
154	    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
155	        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
156	                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
157	                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
158	                  name=$NF;
159	                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
160	              }' \
161	        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true
162	
163	    # Capture disassembly for RVV detection: each line is "<hex-pc> <mnemonic>".
164	    # Works for both GNU binutils objdump and llvm-objdump.
165	    if [[ "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" -eq 1 ]]; then
166	        "$OBJDUMP" -d --no-show-raw-insn "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
167	            | awk '
168	                match($0, /^[ \t]*[0-9a-fA-F]+:[ \t]+[^ \t]+/) {
169	                    line = substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH);
170	                    sub(/:/, " ", line);
171	                    n = split(line, a, /[ \t]+/);
172	                    pc = ""; mn = "";
173	                    for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
174	                        if (a[i] == "") continue;
175	                        if (pc == "") { pc = a[i]; continue; }
176	                        mn = a[i]; break;
177	                    }
178	                    if (pc != "" && mn != "") print pc, mn;
179	                }' > "$DISASM" || true
180	    fi
181	fi
182	
183	# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
184	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
185	    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
186	                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
187	                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
188	        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
189	            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
190	            break
191	        fi
192	    done
193	fi
194	
195	# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
196	# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
197	if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
198	    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
199	    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
200	        # addr2line: -e <elf> -f -s -C reads addrs on stdin, prints
201	        # alternating function/file:line; we ask for file:line only via -p.
202	        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" -s 2>/dev/null || true)"
203	        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
204	    fi
205	fi
206	
207	# --- format ---
208	python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" <<'PY'
209	import sys, re, bisect
210	
211	log_path = sys.argv[1]
212	syms_path = sys.argv[2]
213	lines_path = sys.argv[3]
214	disasm_path = sys.argv[4]
215	limit = int(sys.argv[5])
216	min_iters = int(sys.argv[6])
217	optimize_rvv = int(sys.argv[7]) == 1
218

221	    with open(syms_path) as f:
222	        for line in f:
223	            parts = line.split()

225	                addr = int(parts[0])

253

261	        if m:
262	            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
263	                         int(m.group(2), 16),

268	    sys.exit(0)

272	if optimize_rvv:
273	    # Build a sorted list of PCs whose mnemonic indicates an RVV insn.
274	    # In standard RISC-V, only V-extension mnemonics begin with 'v'
275	    # (vadd, vmul, vle*, vse*, vsetvli, vsetvl, vsetivli, ...).
276	    rvv_pcs = []
277	    try:
278	        with open(disasm_path) as f:
279	            for line in f:
280	                parts = line.split()
281	                if len(parts) < 2:
282	                    continue
283	                try:
284	                    pc = int(parts[0], 16)

287	                mn = parts[1].lower()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Hot inner-loop detector wrapper (system-mode, baremetal RISC-V).
#
# Runs a baremetal ELF under qemu-system-<arch> with the hotloops plugin
# and prints a sorted table of detected backward branches:
#
#   iters | start_pc -> end_pc | function:offset
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/hotloops.sh [opts] <elf>
#
# Options:
#   --arch <riscv32|riscv64|auto>   Target arch (default: auto)
#   --qemu <path>                   Override qemu-system binary
#   --plugin <path>                 Override plugin .so
#   --machine <name>                -M <name> (default andes_ae350)
#   --cpu <spec>                    -cpu <spec>
#                                   (default andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024)
#   --qemu-config <file>            -readconfig <file> (e.g. AE350 device cfg)
#   --cwd <dir>                     chdir before launching QEMU so semihosting
#                                   opens (fread of bin/*.bin) resolve
#   --qemu-arg <arg>                Append an extra arg to the QEMU command
#                                   (repeatable)
#   --limit <N>                     Top-N loops to print (default 50)
#   --min-iters <N>                 Drop loops with fewer than N iters (default 1)
#   --objdump <path>                Override objdump (resolves symbols)
#   --addr2line <path>              Override addr2line (resolves source lines)
#   --no-source                     Disable source file:line resolution
#   --raw                           Print raw plugin output, no formatting
#   --optimize-rvv                  Only report loops with at least one RVV
#                                   instruction (default: on)
#   --no-optimize-rvv               Disable the RVV-only filter
#   -h | --help                     Show help

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

ARCH="auto"
QEMU=""
PLUGIN=""
LIMIT=50
MIN_ITERS=1
OBJDUMP=""
ADDR2LINE=""
NO_SOURCE=0
RAW=0
OPTIMIZE_RVV=1
MACHINE="andes_ae350"
CPU="andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=1024"
QEMU_CONFIG=""
QEMU_CWD=""
EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS=()

usage() { sed -n '2,30p' "$0"; exit 0; }

# --- arg parsing ---
ELF=""
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --arch)        ARCH="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu)        QEMU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --plugin)      PLUGIN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --machine)     MACHINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cpu)         CPU="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-config) QEMU_CONFIG="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --cwd)         QEMU_CWD="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --qemu-arg)    EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS+=("$2"); shift 2 ;;
        --limit)       LIMIT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --min-iters)   MIN_ITERS="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --objdump)     OBJDUMP="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --addr2line)   ADDR2LINE="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --no-source)   NO_SOURCE=1; shift ;;
        --raw)         RAW=1; shift ;;
        --optimize-rvv)    OPTIMIZE_RVV=1; shift ;;
        --no-optimize-rvv) OPTIMIZE_RVV=0; shift ;;
        -h|--help)     usage ;;
        -*) echo "unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 2 ;;
        *)
            if [[ -z "$ELF" ]]; then
                ELF="$1"; shift
            else
                echo "error: unexpected positional arg: $1" >&2; exit 2
            fi ;;
    esac
done

[[ -n "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: ELF path required" >&2; exit 2; }
[[ -f "$ELF" ]] || { echo "error: not found: $ELF" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- arch detection ---
if [[ "$ARCH" == "auto" ]]; then
    file_out="$(file -L -- "$ELF" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    case "$file_out" in
        *"ELF 64-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv64 ;;
        *"ELF 32-bit"*"RISC-V"*)  ARCH=riscv32 ;;
        *) echo "error: cannot auto-detect arch (not RISC-V?), pass --arch" >&2; exit 2 ;;
    esac
fi

# --- locate qemu + plugin ---
if [[ -z "$QEMU" ]]; then
    QEMU="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/qemu-system-${ARCH}"
fi
[[ -x "$QEMU" ]] || { echo "error: qemu not found/executable: $QEMU" >&2; exit 2; }

if [[ -z "$PLUGIN" ]]; then
    PLUGIN="${QEMU_ROOT}/build/contrib/plugins/libhotloops.so"
fi
[[ -f "$PLUGIN" ]] || { echo "error: plugin not built: $PLUGIN" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- run ---
LOG="$(mktemp -t hotloops.XXXXXX.log)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG"' EXIT

QEMU_CMD=("$QEMU"
          -plugin "${PLUGIN},limit=${LIMIT},min_iters=${MIN_ITERS}"
          -d plugin -D "$LOG"
          -M "$MACHINE"
          -cpu "$CPU"
          -nographic -semihosting
          -bios "$ELF")
[[ -n "$QEMU_CONFIG" ]] && QEMU_CMD+=(-readconfig "$QEMU_CONFIG")
[[ ${#EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]} -gt 0 ]] && QEMU_CMD+=("${EXTRA_QEMU_ARGS[@]}")
if [[ -n "$QEMU_CWD" ]]; then
    ( cd "$QEMU_CWD" && "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" ) >/dev/null
else
    "${QEMU_CMD[@]}" >/dev/null
fi

if [[ "$RAW" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cat "$LOG"
    exit 0
fi

# --- symbol table from objdump (best effort) ---
if [[ -z "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-objdump riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump \
                riscv32-unknown-elf-objdump objdump; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            OBJDUMP="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

SYMS="$(mktemp -t hotloops_syms.XXXXXX)"
LINES="$(mktemp -t hotloops_lines.XXXXXX)"
DISASM="$(mktemp -t hotloops_disasm.XXXXXX)"
trap 'rm -f "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM"' EXIT
if [[ -n "$OBJDUMP" ]]; then
    # Capture FUNC symbols: addr size type name
    "$OBJDUMP" -t "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
        | awk 'tolower($0) ~ /[ \t]f[ \t]/ && NF>=5 {
                  addr=strtonum("0x" $1);
                  size=strtonum("0x" $(NF-1));
                  name=$NF;
                  if (size>0) print addr, size, name;
              }' \
        | sort -n -k1,1 > "$SYMS" || true

    # Capture disassembly for RVV detection: each line is "<hex-pc> <mnemonic>".
    # Works for both GNU binutils objdump and llvm-objdump.
    if [[ "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" -eq 1 ]]; then
        "$OBJDUMP" -d --no-show-raw-insn "$ELF" 2>/dev/null \
            | awk '
                match($0, /^[ \t]*[0-9a-fA-F]+:[ \t]+[^ \t]+/) {
                    line = substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH);
                    sub(/:/, " ", line);
                    n = split(line, a, /[ \t]+/);
                    pc = ""; mn = "";
                    for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
                        if (a[i] == "") continue;
                        if (pc == "") { pc = a[i]; continue; }
                        mn = a[i]; break;
                    }
                    if (pc != "" && mn != "") print pc, mn;
                }' > "$DISASM" || true
    fi
fi

# --- locate addr2line for source line resolution (DWARF) ---
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -z "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    for cand in llvm-addr2line addr2line \
                riscv64-unknown-elf-addr2line riscv32-unknown-elf-addr2line \
                riscv64-linux-gnu-addr2line; do
        if command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            ADDR2LINE="$cand"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Pre-collect all PCs from the log and batch-resolve to file:line.
# LINES file format: <hex-pc> <file:line>
if [[ "$NO_SOURCE" -eq 0 && -n "$ADDR2LINE" ]]; then
    pcs="$(awk -F',' '/^0x/ {gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$1); gsub(/[ \t]/,"",$2); print $1; print $2}' "$LOG" | sort -u)"
    if [[ -n "$pcs" ]]; then
        # addr2line: -e <elf> -f -s -C reads addrs on stdin, prints
        # alternating function/file:line; we ask for file:line only via -p.
        out="$(printf '%s\n' $pcs | "$ADDR2LINE" -e "$ELF" -s 2>/dev/null || true)"
        paste <(printf '%s\n' $pcs) <(printf '%s\n' "$out") > "$LINES" || true
    fi
fi

# --- format ---
python3 - "$LOG" "$SYMS" "$LINES" "$DISASM" "$LIMIT" "$MIN_ITERS" "$OPTIMIZE_RVV" <<'PY'
import sys, re, bisect

log_path = sys.argv[1]
syms_path = sys.argv[2]
lines_path = sys.argv[3]
disasm_path = sys.argv[4]
limit = int(sys.argv[5])
min_iters = int(sys.argv[6])
optimize_rvv = int(sys.argv[7]) == 1

syms = []
try:
    with open(syms_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.split()
            if len(parts) >= 3:
                addr = int(parts[0])
                size = int(parts[1])
                name = " ".join(parts[2:])
                syms.append((addr, size, name))
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def resolve(pc):
    for addr, size, name in syms:
        if addr <= pc < addr + size:
            return f"{name}+0x{pc-addr:x}"
    return "?"

src_map = {}
try:
    with open(lines_path) as f:
        for line in f:
            parts = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t", 1)
            if len(parts) == 2:
                pc_str, src = parts
                src = src.strip()
                if src and not src.startswith("??"):
                    try:
                        src_map[int(pc_str, 16)] = src
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
except FileNotFoundError:
    pass

def src_line(pc):
    return src_map.get(pc, "")

rows = []
with open(log_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        m = re.match(r"\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*,\s*(\d+)", line)
        if m:
            rows.append((int(m.group(1), 16),
                         int(m.group(2), 16),
                         int(m.group(3))))

if not rows:
    sys.stderr.write("hotloops: no backward edges observed\n")
    sys.exit(0)

rows = [r for r in rows if r[2] >= min_iters]

if optimize_rvv:
    # Build a sorted list of PCs whose mnemonic indicates an RVV insn.
    # In standard RISC-V, only V-extension mnemonics begin with 'v'
    # (vadd, vmul, vle*, vse*, vsetvli, vsetvl, vsetivli, ...).
    rvv_pcs = []
    try:
        with open(disasm_path) as f:
            for line in f:
                parts = line.split()
                if len(parts) < 2:
                    continue
                try:
                    pc = int(parts[0], 16)
                except ValueError:
                    continue
                mn = parts[1].lower()
                if mn.startswith("v") and len(mn) > 1 and mn[1].isalpha():
                    rvv_pcs.append(pc)
    except FileNotFoundError:
        pass
    rvv_pcs.sort()

    def has_rvv_in_range(s, e):
        if not rvv_pcs or s > e:
            return False
        i = bisect.bisect_left(rvv_pcs, s)
        return i < len(rvv_pcs) and rvv_pcs[i] <= e

    rows = [r for r in rows if has_rvv_in_range(r[0], r[1])]

rows.sort(key=lambda r: -r[2])
rows = rows[:limit]

if not rows:
    msg = f"hotloops: no loops with iters >= {min_iters}"
    if optimize_rvv:
        msg += " containing RVV instructions"
    sys.stderr.write(msg + "\n")
    sys.exit(0)

hdr = f"# hot loops (iters >= {min_iters}"
if optimize_rvv:
    hdr += ", RVV-only"
hdr += f"), sorted desc, top {len(rows)}"
print(hdr)

have_src = bool(src_map)

w_iter = max(len("iters"), max(len(str(r[2])) for r in rows))
w_pc = 18  # 0x + 16 hex
w_sym = max(len("location"),
            max(len(f"{resolve(r[0])} -> {resolve(r[1])}") for r in rows))

if have_src:
    def srcpair(s, e):
        a, b = src_line(s), src_line(e)
        if a and b and a == b:
            return a
        return f"{a or '?'} -> {b or '?'}"
    w_src = max(len("source"),
                max(len(srcpair(r[0], r[1])) for r in rows))
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | "
          f"{'location':<{w_sym}} | source")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym + 3 + w_src))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | "
              f"{loc:<{w_sym}} | {srcpair(s, e)}")
else:
    print(f"{'iters':>{w_iter}} | {'start_pc':<{w_pc}} -> {'end_pc':<{w_pc}} | location")
    print("-" * (w_iter + 3 + w_pc + 4 + w_pc + 3 + w_sym))
    for s, e, c in rows:
        loc = f"{resolve(s)} -> {resolve(e)}"
        print(f"{c:>{w_iter}} | 0x{s:016x} -> 0x{e:016x} | {loc}")
PY

content.text:
Plugin is back to baseline. Only `scripts/hotloops.sh` and `docs/hotloops.md` changed.

Summary:
- Plugin reverted - no TCG changes.
- Script captures objdump disassembly when `--optimize-rvv` is on (default), then filters loops where no PC in `[start_pc, end_pc]` has a mnemonic starting with `v` (the standard RISC-V V-extension prefix: `vadd`, `vle*`, `vsetvli`, ...).
- `--no-optimize-rvv` skips both the disassembly capture and the filter.
- `--raw` still emits the unfiltered plugin output (filter lives in the formatting layer).
- Detection is exact for the loop body: scans every PC in the dynamic range, no pre-loop contamination.

Caveat: the body of a loop may include code that's reached via call/jump from inside the range (e.g., a callee). PCs are checked only within `[start_pc, end_pc]` of the ELF text, so RVV inside a called function isn't counted toward the caller's loop. Acceptable trade-off for "innermost RVV loop".

message.content: how to count a nested loop?

content.text:
Each loop is its own `(start_pc, end_pc)` pair, counted independently by its own backward branch. The plugin doesn't model nesting at all - it just sees backward TB transitions.

Example:

```c
for (i = 0; i < 10; i++)        // outer: backward branch at PC_o
    for (j = 0; j < 100; j++)   // inner: backward branch at PC_i
        ...                      // RVV body
```

What gets counted:
- Inner edge `(start=inner_head, end=PC_i)`: 99 backward branches per outer iteration x 10 outer iterations = **990 iters**. (The last `j` falls through instead of branching back.)
- Outer edge `(start=outer_head, end=PC_o)`: **9 iters**. (Last `i` falls through.)

Both rows appear in the output; the inner is sorted higher because its count dominates.

Caveats specific to nesting:

1. **Counts are multiplicative for inner loops.** Inner shows total inner iterations across all outer iterations, not per-call. To get per-call, divide by the outer count.

2. **Off-by-one per call.** Each entry into a loop loses one iteration in the count (the exit fall-through), so a loop that runs N times shows N-1. For a loop called K times, you lose K, not 1.

3. **RVV filter marks both inner and outer.** The outer loop's range `[outer_head, PC_o]` contains the inner loop's body, which contains RVV. So a scalar outer wrapping an RVV inner gets reported as "has RVV" too. The high iter count on the inner usually makes it obvious which one is the actual RVV loop.

4. **Loops split across function calls aren't merged.** If a hot inner loop lives inside a callee, its `(start_pc, end_pc)` is in the callee, not the caller. The caller's loop range doesn't include the callee's PCs, so the caller won't be flagged for RVV that runs inside calls it makes.

5. **Irreducible / multi-back-edge loops** (e.g. a loop with two `continue` paths to the header at different tail PCs) appear as multiple distinct rows sharing the same `start_pc` but different `end_pc`. Sum them to get the true iteration count of the loop.

is it possible to draw the nested loops when printing?

content.text:
Yes - infer nesting from PC-range containment: loop A is nested in B if `start_B <= start_A` and `end_A <= end_B`. For typical N <= 50 loops, O(N^2) parent-finding is fine.

Two rendering options, with a real tradeoff:

**Option 1 - tree-indented (groups parents+children together, breaks iter-desc order):**
```
iters | start_pc -> end_pc | location                              | source
------+--------------------+---------------------------------------+--------
   10 | 0x1000 -> 0x10c0   | foo+0x10 -> foo+0xd0                  | x.c:5
  990 | 0x1020 -> 0x1080   | `- bar_inline+0x4 -> +0x64            | x.c:7
   10 | 0x1090 -> 0x10b0   | `- bar2+0x0 -> +0x20                  | x.c:10
   50 | 0x2000 -> 0x2080   | baz+0x0 -> +0x80                      | y.c:3
```

**Option 2 - flat table sorted by iters desc + extra `parent` column:**
```
iters | start_pc -> end_pc | parent | location                 | source
------+--------------------+--------+--------------------------+--------
  990 | ...                |    #2  | bar_inline+0x4 -> +0x64  | x.c:7
   50 | ...                |    -   | baz+0x0 -> +0x80         | y.c:3
   10 | 0x1000 -> 0x10c0   |    -   | foo+0x10 -> foo+0xd0     | x.c:5
   10 | 0x1090 -> 0x10b0   |    #2  | bar2+0x0 -> +0x20        | x.c:10
```

Option 1 is more readable but loses the iter-sorted top-line. Option 2 keeps the sort and lets you cross-reference. Both only catch **same-function nesting** - loops across `call` boundaries (caller's hot loop calling a callee with its own hot loop) won't show up as parent/child since callee PCs are outside the caller's range.

message.content: design a way to allow LLM to read and easy to understand the hotest loop

content.text:
Goal: make the output answer "what is the hottest loop and what does it look like?" in the first ~10 lines an LLM reads, with everything else cross-referenced by stable IDs.

Recommended format: **Markdown with a TL;DR + per-loop fact-sheet blocks**, opt-in via `--llm` (keep the existing table as default for humans).

```markdown
# Hotloops Report
target: hotloops_demo  (riscv64, ax45mpv, vlen=1024)
total_backward_branches: 32_500    distinct_edges: 4    rvv_filter: on

## TL;DR
Hottest loop is L1 in `dot` (hotloops_demo.c:6), 31_500 iters = 96.9% of all
taken backward branches. Body is RVV (vle32.v + vmul.vv + vadd.vv). It is
top-level (no parent loop) and contains no inner loop. Everything else is
small scalar startup noise from libgcc.

## L1  (hottest, 96.9%)
iters:        31500
pc_range:     0x1056e .. 0x1057c           (size: 14 B, 4 insns)
function:     dot+0xc -> dot+0x1a
source:       hotloops_demo.c:6
code:         |
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += a[i] * b[i];
rvv:          yes
rvv_insns:
  - 0x10570  vle32.v   v1, (a0)
  - 0x10574  vmul.vv   v2, v1, v3
  - 0x10578  vadd.vv   v4, v4, v2
parent:       -
children:     []
calls:        []                            # callee functions invoked from body

## L2  (5.4%)
iters:        1757
...
```

Why this works for an LLM:

- **TL;DR up front.** The model can answer "what's the hottest loop?" without parsing any tables or doing arithmetic - the percentage, RVV fact, and nesting are all stated in prose.
- **Stable IDs (`L1`, `L2`...).** The user (or follow-up prompts) can say "tell me about L1" and the model knows exactly which row.
- **Self-describing fields.** Every value is `name: value`, no column alignment to misread, no "what does column 4 mean".
- **Source snippet inline** (best-effort, from the `file:line`). Saves the model from needing to open the source file.
- **RVV listed by mnemonic, not just "yes".** Lets the model reason about *which* vector ops dominate without re-disassembling.
- **Nesting expressed as `parent`/`children` IDs**, not visual indentation. Robust for nested or split-back-edge cases.
- **`calls:` field** for callees executed from inside the body - this is the missing link the PC-range nesting can't capture, and it's what an LLM needs to follow the hot path through call boundaries.

Tradeoffs:

1. **Token cost**: ~10x the flat table. Mitigate by capping RVV listing to top-K mnemonics and only emitting the source snippet for the top L loops (e.g. K=8, L=3).
2. **Source snippet** needs the source file resolvable from DWARF paths - if the build path is gone, fall back to just `file:line`.
3. **`calls:` field** requires extra static analysis: scan disassembly in `[start_pc, end_pc]` for `jal`/`jalr` targets and resolve them via the symbol table. Doable from data we already have.
4. Don't try to mix tree-drawing characters into this format - the explicit `parent:`/`children:` references parse cleanly even when an LLM re-emits or summarizes the report.

Want me to implement this as `--llm` mode, with the TL;DR generated from `(iters, pct, parent, rvv_present)` and the per-loop blocks emitted in iter-desc order?

The script does, by template - no LLM call.

It's mechanical prose generated from the same data already in the report:

```python
def render_tldr(loops, total_iters):
    top = loops[0]                            # already sorted iters desc
    pct = 100 * top.iters / total_iters
    rvv_str = ("RVV (" + ", ".join(top.rvv_mnemonics[:3]) + ")"
               if top.has_rvv else "scalar")
    nest_str = ("top-level" if top.parent is None
                else f"nested inside {top.parent.id}")
    child_str = ("contains no inner loop" if not top.children
                 else f"contains {len(top.children)} inner loop(s): "
                      + ", ".join(c.id for c in top.children))
    return (f"Hottest loop is {top.id} in `{top.fn}` ({top.source}), "
            f"{top.iters} iters = {pct:.1f}% of total. "
            f"Body is {rvv_str}. It is {nest_str}, {child_str}.")
```

Two reasons to keep it template-based, not LLM-generated:

1. **The tool must work offline and deterministically.** A CLI that phones home to an LLM service to summarize its own output is fragile and slow. The reproducibility also matters when the report is committed to a perf-tracking repo or diffed across runs.
2. **The data is already factual.** The TL;DR is just `(hottest_id, pct, fn, source, rvv_yes/no, parent_or_top, children)` rendered into a sentence - all values are already computed for the per-loop blocks. There's nothing for an LLM to "infer" that the script doesn't already know.

The downstream LLM that *reads* the report can then synthesize its own higher-level summary across multiple reports, cross-reference with source diffs, etc. - but that's the consumer's job, not the producer's.

content.text:
Implementing `--llm` mode now. Plan:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# hotloops - hot inner-loop detector

Detects the hottest loops in a RISC-V ELF by observing backward
translation-block transitions in QEMU. For each detected loop you get
the loop header PC, the backward-branch PC, and the iteration count.
With DWARF, you also get the source `file:line`.

Two parts ship together:

- `contrib/plugins/hotloops.c` - QEMU TCG plugin that records edges.
- `scripts/hotloops.sh` - wrapper that runs the plugin and formats output.

## Usage

```
scripts/hotloops.sh [options] <elf> [--] [program args...]
```

### Options

| Flag                | Default | Description                                                    |
|---------------------|---------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| `--arch <a>`        | auto    | Target arch: `riscv32`, `riscv64`, or `auto` (from `file`).    |
| `--qemu <path>`     | auto    | Override the QEMU binary.                                      |
| `--plugin <path>`   | auto    | Override the plugin `.so`.                                     |
| `--limit <N>`       | 50      | Print at most N loops.                                         |
| `--min-iters <N>`   | 1       | Drop loops with fewer than N iterations (hot-loop threshold).  |
| `--objdump <path>`  | auto    | Override objdump (for `function+offset` resolution).           |
| `--addr2line <path>`| auto    | Override addr2line (for source line resolution).               |
| `--no-source`       | off     | Skip source resolution even if DWARF is present.               |
| `--raw`             | off     | Print raw plugin output (CSV-ish), no formatting.              |
| `--optimize-rvv`    | on      | Only report loops that contain at least one RVV instruction.   |
| `--no-optimize-rvv` | off     | Disable the RVV-only filter; report all loops.                 |

### Build prerequisites

The plugin lives in `contrib/plugins/`. Build once:

```
ninja -C build contrib-plugins
```

It is incremental on subsequent edits to `hotloops.c`. The QEMU binaries
themselves (`qemu-riscv64`, `qemu-riscv32`) do **not** need to be rebuilt.

## How it detects a loop

For every translated block, the plugin remembers the PC of its last
instruction. When the next-executed block starts at a PC `<=` that
remembered PC, the transition is a backward edge - i.e. a loop iteration.
The pair `(header_pc, tail_pc)` is the key; the count is the number of
iterations.

This catches every loop a real backward branch closes (for/while/do-while,
goto, manually unrolled tails, etc.) without needing to model branch
semantics.

Caveat: each iteration counted is one *taken* backward branch. A loop
that runs N times has N-1 taken backward branches plus one fall-through
on exit, so the reported count is N-1 per call to the enclosing function.

## Output format

Header line, then a fixed-width table sorted by `iters` descending:

```
# hot loops (iters >= <threshold>), sorted desc, top <K>
iters | start_pc           -> end_pc             | location              | source
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
<n>   | 0x<16-hex>         -> 0x<16-hex>         | <fn>+0x<o> -> <fn>+0x<o> | file:line -> file:line
...
```

Columns:

- `iters`     - taken backward-branch count (sorted desc).
- `start_pc`  - PC of the loop header (the branch target).
- `end_pc`    - PC of the backward branch (the loop tail).
- `location`  - `function+offset -> function+offset` from the ELF symbol table.
                Shows `?` if the address is not inside any sized FUNC symbol.
- `source`    - `file:line -> file:line` from DWARF. Collapsed to a single
                `file:line` when both ends map to the same source line.
                Only present when the ELF has debug info and `--no-source`
                is not set.

The `--raw` mode prints the unformatted plugin output instead:

```
hotloops: <distinct> distinct backward edges, <hot> hot (>= <thr> iters), sorted by iterations desc
start_pc, end_pc, iterations
0x..., 0x..., <n>
...
```

## Example

Source:

```c
/* /tmp/hotloops_demo.c */
#include <stdio.h>

__attribute__((noinline))
static int dot(volatile const int *a, volatile const int *b, int n) {
    int s = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += a[i] * b[i];   /* line 6 */
    return s;
}

int main(void) {
    int a[64], b[64];
    for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) { a[i] = i; b[i] = 64 - i; }
    long total = 0;
    for (int outer = 0; outer < 500; outer++)
        total += dot(a, b, 64);                      /* 64 * 500 inner iters */
    printf("total=%ld\n", total);
    return 0;
}
```

Build with debug info and run:

```
$ riscv64-linux-gnu-gcc -O1 -g -static /tmp/hotloops_demo.c -o /tmp/hotloops_demo
$ scripts/hotloops.sh --limit 5 --min-iters 100 /tmp/hotloops_demo
```

Output:

```
# hot loops (iters >= 100), sorted desc, top 5
iters | start_pc           -> end_pc             | location                                                                | source
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
31500 | 0x000000000001056e -> 0x000000000001057c | dot+0xc -> dot+0x1a                                                     | hotloops_demo.c:6 (discriminator 3) -> hotloops_demo.c:6 (discriminator 1)
 1757 | 0x000000000004ab54 -> 0x000000000004abe8 | read_encoded_value_with_base+0x70 -> read_encoded_value_with_base+0x104 | unwind-pe.h:265 -> unwind-pe.h:255
  876 | 0x000000000004bc5e -> 0x000000000004bd00 | classify_object_over_fdes+0x46 -> classify_object_over_fdes+0xe8        | unwind-dw2-fde.c:738 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:732 (discriminator 1)
  876 | 0x000000000004aae4 -> 0x000000000004bcdc | read_encoded_value_with_base+0x0 -> classify_object_over_fdes+0xc4      | unwind-pe.h:203 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:781
  869 | 0x000000000004bcf2 -> 0x000000000004bd64 | classify_object_over_fdes+0xda -> classify_object_over_fdes+0x14c       | unwind-dw2-fde.c:786 -> unwind-dw2-fde.c:793
```

Reading the top row:

- `iters = 31500` - close to the expected `64 * 500 = 32000`; the missing
  500 are the per-call exits (last iter of `dot` falls through instead of
  branching back).
- `start_pc = 0x1056e`, `end_pc = 0x1057c` - the header and backward
  branch of the inner loop.
- `location` - both PCs are inside `dot`, header at `+0xc`, tail at `+0x1a`.
- `source` - both map to `hotloops_demo.c:6`, which is exactly
  `for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += a[i] * b[i];`.

The remaining rows are glibc unwinder loops that ran during static init.
The 500-iter outer loop (`main+0x38 -> main+0x3a`) was filtered out by
`--min-iters 100` - well below the inner loop's contribution.

## Tips

- Strip rarely-interesting libc/startup loops by raising `--min-iters`
  to a value above your background noise floor (e.g. `--min-iters 1000`
  for a workload that runs your hot path millions of times).
- For a very short workload, lower `--min-iters` and raise `--limit`
  to see the full distribution.
- Pass program arguments after `--`, e.g.
  `scripts/hotloops.sh ./prog -- --bench fast`.
- `--raw` is useful when piping into another tool; the format is stable.

| `--no-optimize-rvv` | off     | Disable the RVV-only filter; report all loops.                 |
+| `--llm`             | off     | Emit a Markdown report tuned for LLM consumption (see below).  |

hotloops: <distinct> distinct backward edges, <hot> hot (>= <thr> iters), sorted by iterations desc
start_pc, end_pc, iterations
0x..., 0x..., <n>
...
```

## LLM mode (`--llm`)

Use `--llm` to emit a Markdown report designed for an LLM to ingest. The
report is structured so the model can answer "what is the hottest loop?"
from the first paragraph, then drill down via stable IDs.

Layout:

- A header block: `target`, `total_iters`, `reported_loops`, `min_iters`,
  `rvv_filter`.
- A `## TL;DR` paragraph naming the hottest loop, its function and source
  line, percentage of total iterations, RVV vs scalar, and nesting.
- One `### L<N>` fact-sheet per reported loop (sorted by iters desc), with:
  `iters`, `pc_range`, `function`, `source`, an inline `code:` snippet
  pulled from the resolved source file (best-effort), `rvv:` summary plus
  `rvv_insns:` listing (capped at 8), `parent:` / `children:` (PC-range
  containment - same-function nesting only), and `calls:` listing direct
  callees inside the loop body (`jal`/`jalr` with statically known target).

Limitations:

- Nesting via PC-range containment captures **only same-function nesting**.
  A caller's hot loop that calls a callee containing its own hot loop will
  show as two unrelated loops; follow `calls:` to bridge.
- Indirect calls (`jalr` through a register) appear as `(indirect)`.
- `code:` snippets require the source file to be reachable at the absolute
  path stored in DWARF; cross-compiled or relocated builds may fall back to
  just `file:line`.
